brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +52.1% since Feb 27 — $107-$112 volatile; Goldman warns could exceed 2008 high ($147.50); IEA: largest supply disruption ever
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 21 intraday ~$107-112, volatile. Hit $119.11 intraday Mar 19. Goldman Sachs warned Brent 'likely to exceed 2008 all-time high ($147.50)' if disruptions persist. Worst case: additional $42/bbl through end of 2027. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history.' Source: ICE Futures Europe / CNBC / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +47.4% since Feb 27 — day range $93-$100; 8+ mbd curtailed; Goldman worst case could exceed $147
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 21 intraday ~$98, day range $93-$100. IEA: at least 8 mbd curtailed plus 2 mbd condensates/NGLs. Goldman worst case projects additional $42/bbl through end of 2027. Source: NYMEX / CNBC / Goldman Sachs.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$0.93 / +31.2% since Feb 27 — highest since Oct 2022 — diesel $5.07 — CA $5.34-$5.53
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). AAA Mar 20: $3.91/gal (+31% since war began). Highest since October 2022. Diesel $5.07 (highest since 2022). CA $5.34-$5.53. Georgia suspended fuel taxes. Jones Act waived Mar 18. Source: AAA / EIA / CNN.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -373 pts / -5.4% — 6-month low below 200-day MA 2nd day; VIX 26.78; RSI 23.34 (deep oversold)
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 20 close: 6,506.48 — below 200-day MA (~6,615) for 2nd consecutive day. RSI at 23.34 (deep oversold). Only 43.9% of S&P stocks above their 200-day MAs. VIX at 26.78 (+11.31%). Quadruple Witching day. Goldman: 'equities have not priced in enough risk premium.' HSBC: markets 'pricing in a recession.' Source: NYSE / CNBC / Goldman / HSBC.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1B/day run rate — 8,000+ targets — 130 vessels destroyed — $200B supplemental pending
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 22: CENTCOM confirmed 8,000+ targets struck, 8,000+ combat sorties. Adm. Brad Cooper: 130 Iranian naval vessels destroyed — 'largest elimination of a navy since WWII.' 5,000-lb bunker-busters on Hormuz coastal sites. 50,000+ US troops in CENTCOM AOR. $200B supplemental pending. Source: CENTCOM / Adm. Cooper / Pentagon.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (22 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1B/day ongoing — $200B supplemental pending
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 22 at ~$1B/day. Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct + up to $210B total economic impact. $200B supplemental pending (faces Republican resistance — GOP leaders 'do not believe they have the votes'). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 8,000+ US — 130 vessels destroyed — 8,000+ sorties — ~23,000+ combined est.
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM confirmed 8,000+ US targets by Mar 21 (up from 7,800+ Mar 20). 8,000+ combat sorties flown. 130 Iranian naval vessels destroyed — 'largest elimination of a navy since WWII' per Adm. Cooper. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~23,000+. Source: CENTCOM / Adm. Brad Cooper / IDF.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 KIA confirmed — Sgt. Pennington (26, Glendale KY) announced Day 22
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 7 by enemy fire: 6 at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait (Day 2), 1 died of wounds at Prince Sultan AB (Day 9). 6 killed in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Day 13). Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington (26, Glendale KY) death announced Mar 21. F-35 struck by Iranian ground fire Mar 19 — pilot stable — first confirmed combat damage to F-35. Source: DoD / CENTCOM.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: ~200 total — vast majority minor — 180+ returned to duty
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. ~200 total, vast majority minor. 180+ returned to duty. 50,000+ US service members deployed to CENTCOM AOR. Source: DoD / CENTCOM.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (Hengaw est.)
iranian_killed.note: Hengaw: 5,300 (511 civilian) — Al Jazeera: 1,444 — HRANA: 3,114 — attack rate down 90%
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — significant discrepancies across sources. Hengaw Organization (Mar 17): 5,300 killed (511 civilian, 4,789 military). HRANA (Mar 17): 3,114 deaths. Al Jazeera tracker (~Mar 20): 1,444. Iranian government via TIME (Mar 11): 1,300+. WHO (Mar 11): 1,300+ dead, 9,000+ injured. Iranian missile/drone attack rates down 90% from opening days. 9+ senior officials killed. Source: Hengaw / HRANA / Al Jazeera / WHO / TIME.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,001+ killed, 2,000+ wounded — 1M+ displaced — 118 children — 570+ Hezbollah eliminated
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and IDF ground operations since March 2. 1,001+ killed including 118 children, 2,000+ wounded, 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population). IDF struck 2,000+ Hezbollah targets, killed 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. 12 medical workers killed in Hezbollah health center strike Day 22. New Beirut evacuation orders: Haret Hreik, Ghobeiry, Hadath. Source: Lebanese Health Ministry / OCHA / IDF.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: Iran: up to 3.2M + Lebanon: 1M+ — 24,600 Afghans returned — UNHCR / OCHA
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Iran: 600,000-1M households (up to 3.2M people per UNHCR). Lebanon: ~1M (~19% of population). 125,000+ Lebanese crossed into Syria by Mar 18. 24,600 Afghans returned to Afghanistan since Feb 28. Internet at 4% of normal. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / NetBlocks.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 24,000+ flights — 2,000-3,000 vessels stranded — 21+ vessel attacks — 8+ maritime workers killed
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures. 2,000-3,000 vessels stranded. 20,000-40,000 seafarers stranded. 21+ commercial vessels attacked, 8+ maritime workers killed. JMIC/UKMTO threat level: CRITICAL. War risk insurance at ~5% of vessel value. 22 countries condemn Hormuz closure. Source: IATA / Flightradar24 / IMO / UKMTO.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran: 204 (Red Crescent) inc. ~168 Minab — Lebanon: 118+ — OHCHR
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iranian Red Crescent: 204 children killed including ~168 at Minab girls' school (Day 1, Tomahawk — US confirmed responsible per NYT/Amnesty). Lebanon: 118+ children among 1,001+ total dead. Dimona strike Day 22 hospitalized 12-year-old boy in serious condition. UN investigation ongoing. Source: Iranian Red Crescent / NYT / Amnesty / Lebanese Health Ministry / OHCHR.
